reasoning: The resolution depends on whether there will be at least 1 'Riot' in El Salvador in the 30-day window ending just before March 8 (Feb 6 to March 7, 2026). The historical base rate for riots in El Salvador according to recent ACLED data is extremely low (0.167 average per 30 days, or ~2 riots over the past year) largely due to the ongoing state of exception and gang crackdowns. As of the forecast date (March 1), 23 days of the 30-day window have elapsed. Thorough web searches in both English and Spanish across international and local sources revealed no reports of riots or violent protests occurring in El Salvador between Feb 6 and March 1. The small remaining probability (~6%) accounts for the possibility of a minor unpublicized event having occurred that gets coded as a riot by ACLED or a localized riot occurring in the final 7 days of the window.
